memberdef.name: R0
memberdef.initializer: do { \ BLK0(i); \ u32 t = Block[(i)]; \ (z) += ((((w) & ((x) ^ (y))) ^ (y)) + t + K0 + RotateLeft32((v), 5U)); \ (w) = RotateLeft32((w), 30U); \ } while (FALSE)

briefdescription.para: < R0, R1, R2, R3, R4 are the different intermediate operations used in SHA1 digest calculation

memberdef.name: R1
memberdef.initializer: do { \ BLK(i); \ u32 t = Block[(i) & 15U]; \ (z) += ((((w) & ((x) ^ (y))) ^ (y)) + t + K0 + RotateLeft32((v), 5U)); \ (w) = RotateLeft32((w), 30U); \ } while (FALSE)

memberdef.name: R2
memberdef.initializer: do { \ BLK(i); \ u32 t = Block[(i) & 15U]; \ (z) += (((w) ^ (x) ^ (y)) + t + K1 + RotateLeft32((v), 5U)); \ (w) = RotateLeft32((w), 30U); \ } while (FALSE)

memberdef.name: R3
memberdef.initializer: do { \ BLK(i); \ u32 t = Block[(i) & 15U]; \ (z) += (((((w) | (x)) & (y)) | ((w) & (x))) + t + K2 + RotateLeft32((v), 5U)); \ (w) = RotateLeft32((w), 30U); \ } while (FALSE)

memberdef.name: R4
memberdef.initializer: do { \ BLK(i); \ u32 t = Block[(i) & 15U]; \ (z) += (((w) ^ (x) ^ (y)) + t + K3 + RotateLeft32((v), 5U)); \ (w) = RotateLeft32((w), 30U); \ } while (FALSE)

briefdescription.para: This inline function waits till SHA3 completes its operation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the SHA3 completes its operation
XST_FAILURE - If a timeout has occurred

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_Sha3 *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, const u32 Size, u8 IsLastUpdate)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3DmaTransfer

briefdescription.para: This function transfers data through DMA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Starting address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes
Flag to indicate whether this is the last update or not

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XST_FAILURE In case of an error
Asserts validate the input arguments
Configure the SSS for SHA3 hashing.
Checking the PMC DMA done bit should be enough.
Acknowledge the transfer has completed

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha3 *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, const u32 Size, u8 IsLastUpdate)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3DataUpdate

briefdescription.para: This function updates hash for data block of size <= 512MB.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Starting address of the data, which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes
Flag to indicate whether this is the last update or not

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure
Handle Partial data and non dword aligned data address
Process data of size in multiple of dwords
Handle remaining data during processing of next data chunk or during data padding

briefdescription.para: Generate padding for the NIST SHA-3.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to location where padding is to be applied
Is the length of padding in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XST_FAILURE - On failure
Validates the input arguments

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a XSecure_Sha3 structure with the default values required for operating the SHA3 cryptographic engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Pointer to the XPmcDma instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If initialization was successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XST_FAILURE - On failure
Validate the input arguments
Error out if the dma type is invalid.
Initialize SSS Instance

briefdescription.para: This function notifies the SHA driver at the end of the SHA data update and last update includes padding also.

detaileddescription.para: This function is called before XSecure_Sha3Finish to prevent driver adding the padding.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If last update can be accepted
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
Validate the input arguments
Update IsLastUpdate flag to TRUE

briefdescription.para: This function configures Secure Stream Switch and starts the SHA-3 engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - On failure
Validate the input arguments
Reset SHA3 engine.
Start SHA3 engine.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the SHA3 engine with the input data located at a 64-bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Starting 64 bit address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure in SSS configuration
Validate the input arguments
Update input data to SHA3 engine
Set SHA under reset on failure condition

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha3 *InstancePtr, const u64 InDataAddr, const u32 Size)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3Update

briefdescription.para: This function updates the SHA3 engine with the input data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Starting address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure in SSS configuration
Update input data to SHA3 engine

briefdescription.para: This function updates SHA3 engine with final data which includes SHA3 padding and reads final hash on complete data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If finished without any errors
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If Sha3PadType is other than KECCAK or NIST
Validate the input arguments
Perform SHA3 padding based on IsLastUpdate flag
Status Reset
Check the SHA3 DONE bit.
Status Reset
Read out the Hash in reverse order.
Set SHA under reset

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha3 *InstancePtr, const u64 InDataAddr, const u32 Size, XSecure_Sha3Hash *Sha3Hash)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3Digest

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the SHA-3 digest on the given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Starting address of the data on which sha3 hash should be calculated
Size of the input data
Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If digest calculation done successfully
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XST_FAILURE - If any error from Sha3Update or Sha3Finish
Validate the input arguments
Start SHA3 engine
Status Reset
Update SHA3 engine
Status Reset
Perform padding and read out the hash

briefdescription.para: This function reads the SHA3 hash of the data and it can be called between calls to XSecure_Sha3Update.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - On failure
Validate the input arguments
Read the hash from the registers and store in the local buffer in reverse order

briefdescription.para: This function assigns SHA3 configuration to the InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Instance
Deviceid of the SHA3

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful initialization
XST_FAILURE - On failure

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a XSecure_Sha3 structure with the default values required for operating the SHA3 cryptographic engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Pointer to the XPmcDma instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If initialization was successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter

briefdescription.para: This function configures Secure Stream Switch and starts the SHA-3 engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
SHA mode to be set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function updates the SHA3 engine with the input data located at a 64-bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Starting 64 bit address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure in SSS configuration

briefdescription.para: This function notifies the SHA driver at the end of the SHA data update and last update includes padding also.

detaileddescription.para: Typically called before XSecure_Sha3Finish to prevent driver adding the padding.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If last update can be accepted
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function updates SHA3 engine with final data which includes SHA3 padding and reads final hash on complete data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha3 instance
Address of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure
Size of the hash in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If finished without any errors
XSECURE_SHA3_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid parameter
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If Sha3PadType is other than KECCAK or NIST

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *InstancePtr, XSecure_ShaMode ShaMode, const u64 DataAddr, const u32 DataSize, u64 HashAddr, const u32 HashBufSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaDigest

briefdescription.para: This wrapper function calls SHA3 digest on the given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the void
SHA mode
Starting address of the data on which sha3 hash should be calculated
Size of the input data
Address of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure
Size of the hash in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If digest calculation done successfully
XST_FAILURE - If any error from Sha3Update or Sha3Finish

briefdescription.para: This function assigns SHA configuration to the InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the void instance
DeviceId of Sha engine

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - Upon Success
XST_FAILURE - Upon Failure
XST_SUCCESS - If the initialization is successful
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 SubsystemId, u32 SrcAddrLow, u32 SrcAddrHigh, u32 Size, u32 DstAddrLow, u32 DstAddrHigh)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3UpdateIpi

briefdescription.para: This function handler calls XSecure_Sha3Update64Bit or XSecure_Sha3Finish based on the Continue bit in the command.

parameterdescription.para: Subsystem ID.
Lower 32 bit address of the input data on which hash has to be calculated
Higher 32 bit address of the input data on which hash has to be calculated
Size of the input data in bytes to be updated
Lower 32 bit address of the output data where hash to be stored
Higher 32 bit address of the output data where hash to be stored

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the sha update/fnish is successful
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure
Initializes and starts the sha3 engine
copy hash to provided destination address using DMA

briefdescription.para: This function is used for single sha3digest or for multiple sha3 updates based on the First packet bit and Continue bit in the command.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the command structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the sha Update/Finish/Digest is successful
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure
Calculate the SHA-3 digest on the given input data
Initiate and complete the DMA to DMA transfer

briefdescription.para: This function calls respective IPI handler based on the API_ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the command structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the handler execution is successful
XST_INVALID_PARAM - If cmd is NULL or if invalid API ID is received.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY - If IPI channel is busy
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure
SHA IPI event handling
Call the API handler according to API ID

detaileddescription.para: This is the core of the algorithm.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to SHA1 context
Pointer to 512-bit block data

listitem.para:
XST_FAILURE In case of failure in SHA1 transform function
XST_SUCCESS In case of success in SHA1 transform function
XST_GLITCH_ERROR In case of glitch detected in SHA1 calculation
Copy context->state[] to working variables
Loop unrolled. 80 iteration are unrolled for 4 rounds of 20 operations each.
Store the working context
Clear intermediate variables

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to SHA1 context

listitem.para: Set the initial state of the context

briefdescription.para: Read calculated hash from the context and store in user provided memory.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to SHA1 context
Pointer to memory location where calculated hash to be stored

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS In case of successful read
XST_FAILURE - Upon failure
Read SHA1 hash
Check if all bytes are read

briefdescription.para: This function adds padding to data length.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to memory location where Length is to be padded
SHA1 padding length field in bytes

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *const Data, u32 Len, u8 *const Hash)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha1Digest

briefdescription.para: This function calculates SHA1 hash on given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to input data
Length of input data
Pointer to memory location where calculated hash to be stored

listitem.para:
XST_FAILURE In case of failure in SHA1 digest calculation
XST_SUCCESS In case of successful calculation of SHA1 digest
XSECURE_SHA1_TRANSFORM_ERROR In case of failure in SHA1 transform operation
XST_GLITCH_ERROR In case of glitch detected in SHA1 calculation
Available bytes for padding in last block
Read hash

briefdescription.para: Define XSecure_Sha3Hash with XSecure_Sha384Hash to maintain backward compatibility.
Macro to Rotate left the 32-bit value.
Multiply 32-bit Len by 8 and store 64-bit result in LenHigh, LenLow.

detaileddescription.para: Make sure ByteNo is between 0 to 3.

briefdescription.para: Size of SHA1 hash in bytes.
